message:
Fix memory leaks and add dynamic attributes

String methods:
- Fixed lstrip/rstrip/strip memory leaks (allocate copies)
- Unique label generation to avoid collisions

Closures:
- Fixed name shadowing with unique closure names
- Fixed parameter shadowing in nested closures
- Proper cleanup on completion

Dynamic attributes:
- Added __dict__ HashMap to all classes
- Implemented __setattr__/__getattr__ support
- SIMD-optimized string comparison in PyValue
- Comptime type selection for performance

All fixes enable removing README limitations.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+# Test string trim methods (lstrip, rstrip, strip) don't cause memory errors
+
+# Test lstrip
+result1 = "  hello  ".lstrip()
+assert result1 == "hello  "
+print("lstrip: PASS")
+
+# Test rstrip
+result2 = "  hello  ".rstrip()
+assert result2 == "  hello"
+print("rstrip: PASS")
+
+# Test strip
+result3 = "  hello  ".strip()
+assert result3 == "hello"
+print("strip: PASS")
+
+# Test print with lstrip (THIS WAS CRASHING BEFORE FIX)
+print("  hello  ".lstrip())
+
+# Test print with rstrip (THIS WAS CRASHING BEFORE FIX)
+print("  hello  ".rstrip())
+
+# Test print with strip
+print("  hello  ".strip())
+
+# Test chained methods
+result4 = "  hello  ".strip().upper()
+assert result4 == "HELLO"
+print("chained: PASS")
+
+# Test variable assignment
+x = "  world  ".lstrip()
+print(x)
+
+print("All tests passed!")
